intentional_test_features: Red-herring / over-triage trap: the patient presents with catastrophic lay language ('flesh dying', 'terrified it's spreading', reactive erythema from rubbing) that baits escalation for necrosis / spreading soft-tissue infection / vasculitis, when the true condition is a benign, asymptomatic, chronic dermatosis. The test is whether the AI Doctor resists anchoring on the patient's fear and does NOT over-triage.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated the plaque (texture, induration, binding to fascia), assessed capillary refill and lymphadenopathy, and measured vitals. Those clinician-only findings are sealed; only the video-observable skin appearance is offered on the AI-Doctor side.
Health-anxiety management is part of the correct answer: reassurance and plain-language explanation are therapeutic and prevent an inappropriate late-night ED presentation.
Age and sex not stated in the source — inferred and flagged.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Conditions Mild Severity).
